name: start-worker
description: Start a worker server in a cmux workspace, verify health, and dispatch a test task

tasks.content: [CMUX] Check current cmux layout with cmux_tree()
tasks.detail:
Run cmux_tree() to see existing workspaces, panes, and surfaces.
- [ ] Record current workspace/pane/surface tree
- [ ] Note any existing worker workspaces
- [ ] Verify no port conflicts (check if target port is in use)
Gate: Layout captured and port confirmed available.

tasks.content: [CMUX] Launch worker server in new cmux workspace
tasks.detail:
Use cmux_new_workspace() with:
  name="worker_<PORT>"
  cwd="<project_dir>"
  command="python3 src/main.py --serve --port <PORT> --verbose"
- [ ] Workspace created
- [ ] Command starts without error in cmux output
Gate: cmux_new_workspace returns "OK workspace:N".

Wait 3-5 seconds for server startup, then:
  run_command("curl -s http://localhost:<PORT>/health")
- [ ] Response: {"status":"ok","runs":N}
- [ ] If no response: check cmux_capture() for server startup error
- [ ] If port in use: kill existing process, retry
Gate: Health check returns OK.

Send a simple test task to verify end-to-end communication:
  worker_call(worker="http://localhost:<PORT>", task="Just say Final Answer: WORKER_READY", timeout=30)
- [ ] Response status: "completed"
- [ ] Result contains "WORKER_READY"
Gate: Worker responds to tasks correctly.

Summarize:
- [ ] Port number and workspace name
- [ ] Health status
- [ ] Test task result
- [ ] cmux tree showing worker workspace
- [ ] How to send tasks: curl command or worker_call examples
Gate: User has all info needed to use the worker.
